decisions.rationale: The official MTA B57 row binds a realized August 31, 2025 onset, the B57 route, and one canonical stop-removal treatment in the same evidence block. This occurrence preserves the literal bus_stop_or_boarding family and does not invent a broader atomic route_redesign treatment or inherit any other Queens route scope.

The official MTA B62 row binds a realized August 31, 2025 onset and one uniquely anchored route to all 2 canonical treatment members stated for that row. Each member retains its literal treatment family. The official Queens Bus Network Redesign project context separately supports route_redesign as the reviewed bundle analysis family. No alternative route scope is inherited.
Migration approval composed from accepted operational-anchor reviews: m86-2015-07-13-off-board-fare, m86-2015-07-13-real-time-information.

The official MTA Q1 row binds one realized onset and route to a three-member service-change package: a western extension, stop removals, and replacement of the prior Springfield Boulevard segment by Q36 service. Canonical members retain their literal service_pattern or bus_stop_or_boarding families. The official Queens Bus Network Redesign project context separately supports route_redesign as the reviewed bundle analysis family. Q36 is replacement context inside one treatment and is not an occurrence route.

The official MTA Q10 row binds a realized August 31, 2025 onset and one uniquely anchored route to exactly two canonical treatment members: one bus_stop_or_boarding change and one service_pattern change. The official Queens Bus Network Redesign project context separately supports route_redesign as the reviewed bundle analysis family. No other route scope is inherited.
